                      have h24 : η.arrow X ≫ η.arrow Y = F.homF f ≫ G.homF f := h1
                      have h25 : (η.arrow X).inv ≫ (η.arrow X) = 1 _ := by
                        apply IsIso.inv_comp
                        exact hX
                      have h26 : (η.arrow Y).inv ≫ (η.arrow Y) = 1 _ := by
                        apply IsIso.inv_comp
                        exact hY
                      have h27 : (F.homF f) ≫ (F.homF f).inv = 1 _ := by
                        apply IsIso.comp_inv
                        exact h2
                      have h28 : (G.homF f) ≫ (G.homF f).inv = 1 _ := by
                        apply IsIso.comp_inv
                        exact h3
                      -- Use the inverses to find the required composition
                      calc
                        (η.arrow X).inv ≫ F.homF f = (η.arrow X).inv ≫ (F.homF f) := rfl
                        _ = (η.arrow X).inv ≫ (F.homF f) := rfl
                        _ = (G.homF f) ≫ (η.arrow Y).inv := by
                          -- Use the fact that the composition of the arrows is the composition of the functors to find the inverse
                          simp_all [Category.comp_assoc, IsIso.inv_comp, IsIso.comp_inv]
                          <;>
                          (try simp_all [IsIso.inv_comp, IsIso.comp_inv]) <;>
                          (try simp_all [Category.comp_assoc]) <;>
                          (try simp_all [IsIso.inv_comp, IsIso.comp_inv]) <;>
                          (try simp_all [Category.comp_assoc]) <;>
                          (try simp_all [IsIso.inv_comp, IsIso.comp_inv]) <;>
                          (try simp_all [Category.comp_assoc]) <;>
                          (try simp_all [IsIso.inv_comp, IsIso.comp_inv]) <;>
                          (try simp_all [Category.comp_assoc])
                          <;>
                          (try aesop)
    -- Use the result to show the required composition
    calc
      (have this := h X; (fun f hf => f.inv) (Classical.choose (show Nonempty (Σ (f : D.Hom (F.F X) (G.F X)), IsIso f) from ⟨⟨η.arrow X, hX⟩, by simp [hX]⟩)) (Classical.choose_spec (show Nonempty (Σ (f : D.Hom (F.F X) (G.F X)), IsIso f) from ⟨⟨η.arrow X, hX⟩, by simp [hX]⟩))).comp (F.homF f) = ((η.arrow X).inv).comp (F.homF f) := by rfl
      _ = (G.homF f).comp ((η.arrow Y).inv) := by
        rw [h4]
        <;> simp [Category.comp_assoc]
      _ = (have this := h Y; (fun f hf => f.inv) (Classical.choose (show Nonempty (Σ (f : D.Hom (F.F Y) (G.F Y)), IsIso f) from ⟨⟨η.arrow Y, hY⟩, by simp [hY]⟩)) (Classical.choose_spec (show Nonempty (Σ (f : D.Hom (F.F Y) (G.F Y)), IsIso f) from ⟨⟨η.arrow Y, hY⟩, by simp [hY]⟩))).comp (F.homF f) := by rfl

simp_all [comp_assoc]
          <;> simp_all [IsIso]
          <;> try contradiction
          <;> try simp_all [IsIso]
          <;> try aesop
    -- Conclude the proof by using the properties of the inverse isomorphism
    have h₇ : (η.arrow Y).inv ≫ F.homF f = G.homF f ≫ (η.arrow X).inv := h₆
    -- Use the properties of the inverse isomorphism to get the final result
    have h₈ : (Classical.choose (h X)).inv ≫ F.homF f = G.homF f ≫ (Classical.choose (h Y)).inv := by
      simpa [hX, hY] using h₇
    simp_all [hX, hY]
    <;> try contradiction
    <;> try simp_all [IsIso]
    <;> try aesop
